name: Micronesia
avoid: May–November is wetter across the FSM, so visibility, hiking comfort, and small-boat reliability are weaker than the drier December–April window.
United's inter-island schedule is infrequent and delays ripple quickly across the Islands Hopper network, so keep buffer time before any long-haul connection.
Cards work at some hotels, but entrance gates, village guides, taxis, and boat skippers often expect cash in US dollars.
stopoverNote: For trips under 6 days, pick Pohnpei plus either Chuuk for diving or Yap for culture; trying all three islands becomes flight-heavy.

cities.name: Pohnpei
cities.notes: Pohnpei is Micronesia's lush historical anchor, mixing Kolonia's practical base with Nan Madol, waterfalls, reef outings, and easy United island-hopper access.

cities.name: Chuuk
cities.notes: Chuuk revolves around Truk Lagoon: world-class WWII wreck diving, Weno-based resort logistics, and short boat rides to both wrecks and cultural stops.

cities.name: Yap
cities.notes: Yap balances stone money villages, manta-ray channels, and a slower cultural rhythm than the rest of the FSM, with Colonia as the main practical base.

itinerary.sim: FSMTC is the closest thing to a local prepaid option, but coverage and top-ups are patchy; assume hotel Wi-Fi plus offline maps and downloaded boarding passes.

Pohnpei.name: Pohnpei
Pohnpei.note: Pohnpei works best as the history-and-nature start: give Nan Madol its own daylight block, then use a second day for waterfalls or a reef boat outing.

days.theme: Kolonia Arrival, Nan Madol and a Waterfall Detour

activities.name: Nan Madol ruins visit
activities.tip: Recent visitor reports usually place entry around US$5–10 per person; carry cash and go with a driver-guide if you want the Saudeleur history explained well.

activities.name: Kepirohi Waterfall stop
activities.tip: The local gate fee is typically about US$3–5 and is easiest to pay in small notes.

activities.name: Spanish Wall and Kolonia waterfront walk
Taxi or driver hire for Nan Madol circuit

days.theme: Reef Day to Ant Atoll or a Slower Rainforest Loop

activities.name: Ant Atoll snorkel or dive boat trip
activities.tip: Day-boat pricing commonly starts near US$90 and rises with gear, lunch, and diver/non-diver mix.

activities.name: Sokehs Ridge or local rainforest guide
Kolonia market fruit and snack stop

Chuuk.name: Chuuk
Chuuk.note: Chuuk rewards divers with at least one full wreck day and one lighter topside or second-dive day; non-divers should still use boat time to see the lagoon from the water.

days.theme: Weno Arrival, Lagoon Orientation and WWII Stories

activities.name: Weno and Tonowas WWII land tour
activities.tip: Half-day hotel-arranged land tours usually land around US$50–80 with a driver and local guide.

activities.name: Truk Lagoon shoreline snorkel or kayak session
Sunset drinks at Blue Lagoon or Truk Stop

days.theme: Full Wreck-Dive Day in Truk Lagoon

activities.name: Two-tank Truk Lagoon wreck diving day
activities.tip: Most resort operators quote roughly US$170–220 for a two-tank day; deeper wrecks often require advanced certification, logged experience, and extra gas charges.

activities.name: Non-diver lagoon boat or shallow wreck snorkel

Yap.name: Yap
Yap.note: Yap is strongest when split between one cultural land-and-boat day and one manta-focused dive or snorkel day instead of rushing around multiple villages in one afternoon.

days.theme: Stone Money Banks, Village Trails and Rumung Views

activities.name: Stone money bank and village land tour
activities.tip: Hotel-arranged half-day cultural tours commonly price near US$50–90 with guide, vehicle, and refreshments included.

activities.name: Rumung Island boat-and-walk tour
activities.tip: The short boat ride is usually bundled into a pre-arranged community tour; ask your hotel to confirm if lunch or coconut service is included.

activities.name: Bechiyal Cultural Center or Colonia sunset stop

days.theme: Mi'il Channel Mantas and a Slower Colonia Evening

activities.name: Two-tank manta ray diving in Yap
activities.tip: Expect around US$150–210 for a two-tank boat trip depending on operator and gear; December to April usually offers the most reliable manta encounters.

activities.name: Tamilyog Trail or guided nature walk

connections.method: Short taxi ride or pre-arranged hotel transfer from the airport into Kolonia-side stays.
Road transfer to Nan Madol by taxi or hired driver; Ant Atoll requires a pre-booked speedboat or snorkel charter.
United Island Hopper flight between the two islands; book early because seats are limited and weather delays can cascade across the route.
Resort dive boats handle almost all lagoon access, with departures from Weno-side docks or resort jetties.
United routing via Guam is the most common link; same-day through itineraries exist on some schedules, but buffers help because missed connections are costly here.
Hotel-arranged boats or dive skiffs cover Rumung, Mi'il Channel, and other water-based day trips from Colonia.

extras: Micronesia uses the US dollar, so all prices here are converted at roughly ₹83 per US$1 and work best as planning ranges rather than guaranteed walk-up rates.
Because United's island-hopper flights may operate only a few times each week on some links, keep at least one spare day before any international flight home.
Many historic, village, and dive activities are easiest to book through your hotel rather than online because local operators often confirm by phone, WhatsApp, or email.
Carry reef shoes, mosquito repellent, and a dry bag: Nan Madol paths can be muddy, rain showers are common even in the drier months, and most boat transfers stay wet.
